data.title: Test gate observability: detect irrelevant test→source mappings
data.description:
## Context

The pre-commit test gate (pre-commit-test-gate.sh) enforces test→source mappings via fuzzy matching and .test-index. There is currently no visibility into whether these mappings are accurate — the gate may require tests that are not relevant to the staged changes, causing false positives and unnecessary test runs.

## Success Criteria

1. Per-commit structured logging shows which source files triggered which test requirements, including the match method (fuzzy vs .test-index)
2. A periodic mapping quality analysis tool flags "phantom mappings" — test files that match by name but don't actually exercise the mapped source file
3. False-positive detection compares gate-required tests vs tests that actually exercise changed code

## Approach

Three levels of observability, implementable incrementally:

**Level 1 — Per-commit logging (lightweight):** Add structured log lines to pre-commit-test-gate.sh for each source→test mapping enforced: TEST_GATE_MATCH: source=<path> method=<fuzzy|test-index> test=<path>

**Level 2 — False-positive detection (medium):** After gate passes and commit succeeds, compare files the gate required tests for vs files whose tests actually exercise the changed code (via coverage or behavioral change detection).

**Level 3 — Mapping quality score (systematic):** Periodic script that checks whether each .test-index entry and fuzzy match corresponds to a test that actually imports/sources/executes the mapped source file. Reports mapping accuracy percentage and flags phantom mappings.

## Dependencies

None. Independent of w22-jucp (rebase-aware test gate).
